articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
articles.description: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

images.name: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
images.caption: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

article.headline: Argentina thrash Curacao on another record night for Lionel Messi
article.description: Argentina continued their post-World Cup celebrations with an runaway 7-0 friendly win over Curacao on another memorable night for Lionel Messi.
article.linkText: Argentina score 7 as post-WC party rolls on

article.story:
Argentina continued their post-World Cup celebrations with a runaway 7-0 friendly win over Curacao Tuesday on another memorable night for talisman Lionel Messi.

Messi put on a clinic in the first half, scoring a hat trick to surpass the 100 goals mark for Argentina, with Nicolas Gonzalez and Enzo Fernandez both netting before the break as the rout was well and truly on in Santiago del Estero.

- Messi milestone tracker: The records fall every time he plays
- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga, more (U.S.)

It was the second match in a row in which Messi surpassed a significant milestone in his illustrious career after hitting the 800-goal mark for club and country in a 2-0 win against Panama on Thursday.

An overmatched Curacao put up a fight for the first 20 minutes, but once Messi scored his first the floodgates opened and Gonzalez headed in Argentina's second from close range after a scramble in front of Eloy Room's goal.

The Paris Saint-Germain star soon scored his second and Argentina's third with a trademark, bending shot from his left foot before setting up teammate Fernandez for a thunderous strike from distance to make it 4-0.

Minutes later, Messi was at it again, running on to a perfectly weighted ball over the top of the Curacao defence from Giovani Lo Celso and rolling his shot past a helpless Room to complete a first-half hat trick.

The hosts seemed content to see out the match at a canter in the second half, but Argentina did add to the scoreline in the 78th minute with an Angel Di Maria penalty and a close-range strike from Gonzalo Montiel.

The game was followed by tributes from the home crowd for Lionel Scaloni and his 2022 World Cup winners, who will regroup in June for another international break with an eye on the start of CONMEBOL qualifying for the 2026 World Cup in September.

keyEvents.text: Goal!  Argentina 1, Curaçao 0. Lionel Messi (Argentina) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Giovani Lo Celso.
Goal!  Argentina 2, Curaçao 0. Nicolás González (Argentina) header from very close range to the top left corner    following a corner.
Goal!  Argentina 3, Curaçao 0. Lionel Messi (Argentina) left footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Nicolás González.
Goal!  Argentina 4, Curaçao 0. Enzo Fernández (Argentina) right footed shot from outside the box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Lionel Messi.

Goal!  Argentina 5, Curaçao 0. Lionel Messi (Argentina) left footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Giovani Lo Celso with a through ball   following a fast break.
Substitution, Argentina. Rodrigo De Paul replaces Alexis Mac Allister.
Substitution, Argentina. Exequiel Palacios replaces Enzo Fernández.
Substitution, Argentina. Juan Foyth replaces Nicolás Otamendi.
Substitution, Curaçao. Richairo Zivkovic replaces Rangelo Janga.
Substitution, Curaçao. Jeremy Antonisse replaces Kenji Gorré.
Substitution, Argentina. Ángel Di María replaces Giovani Lo Celso.
Substitution, Argentina. Paulo Dybala replaces Lautaro Martínez.
Substitution, Curaçao. Giovanni Troupée replaces Roshon van Eijma.
Substitution, Curaçao. Xander Severina replaces Brandley Kuwas.
Goal!  Argentina 6, Curaçao 0. Ángel Di María (Argentina) converts the penalty with a left footed shot to the bottom left corner.
Substitution, Argentina. Franco Armani replaces Emiliano Martínez.
Rodrigo De Paul (Argentina) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Vurnon Anita (Curaçao) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Curaçao. Godfried Roemeratoe replaces Vurnon Anita.
Substitution, Curaçao. Kevin Felida replaces Leandro Bacuna.
Goal!  Argentina 7, Curaçao 0. Gonzalo Montiel (Argentina) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the high centre of the goal. Assisted by Paulo Dybala.
